notes: Second validated clue batch compiled from official school websites and other reputable public sources when official site details were sparse.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its homepage tagline quotes Dr. Ernest Boyer: "To help each child build a life as if it were a work of art."
Its mission says the school uses the principles of The Basic School to create an optimal environment for learning.
Official philosophy pages say students are motivated through hands-on teaching, integrated curriculum, and real-life learning with meaningful community connections.
Its school community language emphasizes "the life of the mind," "the vitality of the arts," and "the importance of community."
Student life pages feature both Service Learning and a student publication called the Spartan Sentinel.
Official staff navigation includes a separate RIPPLE staff directory alongside lower and upper school directories.
Its introduction says the school develops the whole child by addressing cognitive, emotional, social, and physical needs.
Official language says every child can and will succeed in ways that reflect individual aptitudes, interests, and healthful lifestyle practices.
The school highlights that it received national accreditation from Cognia.
Its strategic priorities page says mission, vision, values, and the Portrait of a Graduate will be the school's compass.
A student quote featured on the site says, "I don't want to leave school."
Families can also find a separate Camp Corvian link on the official site.
Its homepage features this promise: the school is dedicated to giving all students an opportunity to reach their fullest potential.
That homepage statement is attributed to Anthony Pirrello.
An official news article says Anthony Pirrello was hired as Executive Director effective November 1, 2022.
Its governance section is labeled EWA Board of Directors and includes a dedicated board directory page.
The staff directory lists Bradley Bowman as Athletic Director.
Official athletics pages brand the sports program separately as EWA Athletics.
Its official motto is "Together we learn, lead and serve."
The school says it is more than a school and is a community built on learning, leadership, and service.
Its mission emphasizes developing college-ready learners and responsible leaders with a strong sense of social responsibility.
A signature framework on the site is called The Knight Standard.
Three subpages under that framework are named Future Ready, Thrive, and Leadership in Action.
The parent hub includes a tradition page called "Painting the Schools' Rocks."
Student life includes a scheduling tool called HS FlexiSched.
Its site-wide motto is simply "Choose Joy."
Its mission centers on developing the whole child through instruction, school-community partnerships, and positive self-identity.
The vision says every child should find joy in developing academic potential and thriving as a leader in the community.
Official history language says it stands among the longest-standing charter schools in the state.
Its board page says regular board meetings are held virtually and that public comments require sign-up 24 hours in advance.
Reginald Johnson is listed as board president.
A 2026 school news release announced Dr. Kelvin Bullock as Executive Director.
Its mission statement says the school challenges college-bound students in a creative and supportive atmosphere to become knowledgeable, thoughtful, contributing citizens.
The school's preamble says it aims to sustain individual self-determination and community self-government.
Official mission language says the school graduates citizens of the world through an interconnected learning environment.
Its lessons are described as active, social, and creative.
Its board includes alumni among its members, including Rob Fenoglio and Alex Karsten.
The board page says Lisa Huddleston has served the school as community liaison, mathematics teacher, and academic dean before becoming principal.
A public profile for this school says its mission is to prepare students to become productive citizens who can solve complex problems and think critically.
That same public mission language says students should exhibit positive social competencies and function in a highly technological and global society.
A public school profile says the vision relies on project-based learning to prepare students for STEM-related careers.
One public-facing description says the school believes real-world experiences in a safe and orderly learning environment help students make a positive difference in their communities.
A public profile for the school uses the slogan "Think Big... Think RAM!"
Natasha Wayne is listed in a public volunteer profile as Director of Community Engagement.
Its official messaging says the school builds more than scholars and aims to shape citizens.
The school says its approach combines rigorous academics with timeless values, civic responsibility, and love of country.
Its board page says community members must submit public comment topics by 5 PM the day before a regular board meeting.
Houston Core is listed as chairman on the board page.
The academic technology page says Google Classroom is the school's preferred learning management system.
That same technology page says cell phones are prohibited during the academic day.
Its athletic identity is the Wolves.
The school envisions an extraordinary educational journey that leads to a life of leadership and service.
Its mission says students should become young citizens with a solid academic foundation, a passion for learning, and exemplary character.
The site's values include academic excellence rooted in classical liberal arts and modern science.
Another value statement says the school values strong relationships marked by mutual respect and trust.
The school also highlights a solar array among its official about pages.
Its official motto is the Latin phrase "Esse Quam Videri."
The school says it provides a joyful learning environment while cultivating good character and strong reading, writing, and arithmetic skills.
Official materials repeatedly describe the school as rigorous, classical, and college-preparatory.
Its values page says students begin the day with a Phoenix Pledge and are expected to live "On FIRE" behavior.
The FIRE acronym stands for Fortitude, Integrity, Respect, and Excellence.
Larry Henson is identified on official pages as Executive Director and founder.
John Allen is listed as chairman of the board of directors.
